subtitle: Track books, build your reading habit

views.name: DashboardView
views.purpose: Main hub showing currently reading books with progress bars, today's reading stats (minutes and pages), streak counter with flame icon, and yearly book goal progress ring. Cards for each in-progress book show cover color by genre, title, author, page progress bar, and estimated finish date.

views.name: EntrySheet
views.purpose: Log a reading session — select existing book from picker or add a new book inline with title/author/genre/totalPages. Update current page with stepper, log session duration in minutes, optionally change status (mark finished/abandoned), set rating if finishing, and add notes or quotes.

views.name: HistoryView
views.purpose: Chronological reading sessions grouped by date with calendar heatmap at top showing reading activity intensity. Each session row shows book title, pages read in session, duration, and any notes. Filter by book or genre. Swipe to delete.

views.name: AnalyticsView
views.purpose: Rich analytics: weekly reading minutes line chart, monthly books-finished bar chart, genre distribution pie chart, reading pace trend (pages/min over time), best reading days heatmap by weekday, personal records section (fastest book, longest streak, most pages in a day), and dynamic AI-style insights.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: todayMinutesRead
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of minutesRead for all entries dated today

viewModelComputedProperties.name: todayPagesRead
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of page increments for today's sessions (each session's currentPage minus prior session's currentPage for same book)

viewModelComputedProperties.name: currentStreak
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Count of consecutive days ending today (or yesterday) with at least one reading session of 5+ minutes

viewModelComputedProperties.name: longestStreak
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Maximum consecutive-day reading streak across all history

viewModelComputedProperties.name: booksFinishedThisYear
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Count of unique bookTitles with at least one entry with status == .finished in current calendar year

viewModelComputedProperties.name: totalPagesThisYear
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of all page increments across all sessions in the current calendar year

viewModelComputedProperties.name: currentlyReadingBooks
viewModelComputedProperties.type: [(title: String, author: String, genre: BookGenre, currentPage: Int, totalPages: Int, progressPercent: Double)]
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Unique books whose latest entry has status .currentlyReading, with most recent currentPage and totalPages to compute progress percentage

viewModelComputedProperties.name: yearlyGoalProgress
viewModelComputedProperties.description: booksFinishedThisYear / yearlyBookGoal (default 24), clamped to 0...1 for the progress ring

viewModelComputedProperties.name: weeklyReadingTrend
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Last 7 days with daily totals of minutes read and pages read, for sparkline/bar chart

viewModelComputedProperties.name: averagePagesPerDay
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Mean pages read per day over last 30 days that had at least one session

viewModelComputedProperties.name: readingPace
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Weighted average of (pages read / minutes read) across all sessions where minutesRead > 0, representing pages per minute

viewModelComputedProperties.name: genreDistribution
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Count of unique books per genre with percentage of total library for pie chart

viewModelComputedProperties.name: monthlyBooksChart
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Number of books finished per month for the last 12 months, for bar chart

viewModelComputedProperties.name: averageRating
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Mean rating across all entries where status == .finished and rating > 0

viewModelComputedProperties.name: estimatedFinishDates
viewModelComputedProperties.description: For each currently-reading book: remaining pages / average daily pages for that book = estimated days to finish

viewModelComputedProperties.name: insights
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Dynamic insight strings: streak milestones, pace changes, genre diversity, best reading day, completion rate, monthly comparisons

dailyGoal.description: Daily reading time target in minutes — shown as progress bar on dashboard

yearlyBookGoal.description: Number of books to finish this calendar year — shown as hero progress ring

domainLogic.streakDefinition: A day counts toward the streak if the user logged at least one reading session with minutesRead >= 5. Gap days break the streak. Streak counts backward from today (or yesterday if no session yet today).
domainLogic.trendAnalysis: Compare this week's total reading minutes to last week's total. Compute 7-day rolling average of pages/day vs 30-day average. If 7-day > 30-day by 20%+, show 'Reading pace accelerating' insight. If 7-day < 30-day by 20%+, show 'Reading pace slowing down'. Track pages-per-minute across sessions to detect speed improvements over time.
insights: Reading pace trend: compare average pages/minute of last 5 sessions vs previous 5 — report speeding up, steady, or slowing down
Genre diversity score: count distinct genres read in last 90 days / total genres available — suggest exploring new genres if < 30%
Estimated finish date for each in-progress book based on that book's specific average daily page rate
Monthly volume comparison: this month's pages vs personal best month — celebrate if approaching or exceeding record
Streak milestone celebrations at 7, 14, 21, 30, 50, 100, 200, 365 consecutive days
Completion rate: finished / (finished + abandoned) as percentage — 'You finish 85% of books you start'
Best reading day: weekday with highest average minutesRead — 'Sundays are your power reading day (avg 45 min)'
Reading consistency: standard deviation of daily minutes over last 30 days — lower = more consistent, surface as encouragement
